2014-12-01.title: Tabaré Vázquez Reclaims Presidency in Uruguay Election
2014-12-01.abstract: Voters chose Dr. Vázquez, who served as president from 2005-10, over Luis Lacalle Pou, whose conservative National Party had vowed to scale back the legalization of marijuana.

2014-12-01.title: Russian Money Suspected Behind Fracking Protests
2014-12-01.abstract: Circumstantial evidence, plus large dollops of Cold War-style suspicion, have added to mounting alarm over covert Russian meddling to block threats to its energy stranglehold on Europe.

2014-12-01.title: Ebola Now Preoccupies Once-Skeptical Leader in Guinea
2014-12-01.abstract: President Alpha Condé, whose bureaucracy once played down the Ebola epidemic to avoid scaring investors, has become aggressively involved in the fight.

2014-12-01.title: Straining to Make Room for Refugees as the War in Syria Floods the World
2014-12-01.abstract: Germany has used tents and modified shipping containers to house some of the more than 200,000 people who are expected to apply for asylum there this year.

2014-12-01.title: Nigeria: Mosque Attack’s Toll Rises
2014-12-01.abstract: Nigerian authorities on Sunday significantly raised the death toll from the attack on the central mosque in Kano during prayers on Friday.

2014-12-02.title: Boko Haram Attacks With Ease, Causing Death and Mayhem in Nigeria’s Provinces
2014-12-02.abstract: Two attacks on Monday in Maiduguri and Damaturu, state capitals in northern Nigeria, were among a wave of assaults showing Boko Haram’s ability to strike at will, especially against civilians.

2014-12-02.title: Early Elections Looming in Israel
2014-12-02.abstract: Amid acrimony, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s coalition appears to be on the brink of collapse.

2014-12-02.title: Putin Scraps European Gas Pipeline
2014-12-02.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia announced today that Russia is dropping plans for the South Stream pipeline and instead will expand a pipeline through Turkey.

2014-12-02.title: Three Detained in India in Harassment of Sisters Who Fought Back
2014-12-02.abstract: The young men are accused of sexually harassing two sisters on a bus in the Indian state of Haryana in an incident caught on video.

2014-12-02.title: Lessons From an Ancient Time When Recyclers Walked the Earth
2014-12-02.abstract: Archaeologists working at Qesem Cave in Israel discovered tools that indicated a prehistoric commitment to limiting waste.

2014-12-03.title: A Village Has What All of Italy Wants: The Internet
2014-12-03.abstract: Italy has one of the lowest rates of Internet usage in Europe, but a professor’s experiment is proving that wireless connection can make a difference in one hilltop village.

2014-12-03.title: New Concerns Over Response to Ebola Crisis
2014-12-03.abstract: Doctors Without Borders, which responded early to the outbreak in West Africa, said local doctors, nurses and charity workers were still carrying an inordinate burden.

2014-12-03.title: Egypt Sentences Nearly 200 to Die in Mass Trial Over Killings of Police
2014-12-03.abstract: A court in Giza issued a death sentence to a group of people accused of being supporters of the Muslim Brotherhood and charged in the killings of 11 officers last August.

2014-12-03.title: A Gay Mayor in Poland? No Big Deal
2014-12-03.abstract: The victory of a gay candidate amounts to a sea change in conservative Poland, where the Catholic Church wields enormous political power.

2014-12-03.title: From Particle Physics to the Pentagon
2014-12-03.abstract: Ashton B. Carter, a theoretical physicist and former deputy defense secretary, is President Obama’s choice to be the next defense secretary.

2014-12-04.title: Turkish Police to Get More Search Powers
2014-12-04.abstract: A bill passed by the Turkish Parliament gives the police wider latitude to conduct searches and the government greater control over the judiciary.

2014-12-04.title: Number of Migrants Killed While Fleeing Home Countries Soars
2014-12-04.abstract: The International Organization for Migration said nearly 5,000 have died in journeys for a better life, a majority in the Mediterranean.

2014-12-04.title: Amid Bugs, Hail, Floods and Bacteria, Italian Olives Take a Beating
2014-12-04.abstract: The olive fruit fly and various weather problems helped make 2014 Italian olive oil’s annus horribilis.

2014-12-04.title: Colombia and Rebels Agree to Talks
2014-12-04.abstract: After the release of an army general captured by rebel fighters, the government of Colombia and the country’s largest guerrilla group said on Wednesday that they would soon resume peace talks.

2014-12-04.title: British Regulator Urges Home Births Over Hospitals for Uncomplicated Pregnancies
2014-12-04.abstract: Women with uncomplicated pregnancies are better off with midwives, as hospitalization increased the chances of surgical intervention and infection, guidelines suggested.

2014-12-05.title: Chechen Leader Instagrams Battle With Militants
2014-12-05.abstract: Ramzan Kadyrov, the Kremlin-appointed leader of Chechnya, took time out during an attack by Islamist militants to update his Instagram followers.

2014-12-05.abstract: Pearl of Hope was shut down after the Paris Prosecutor’s office charged its two senior leaders with using charity deliveries as a front to funnel funds to jihadists.

2014-12-05.title: Narendra Modi, Favoring Growth in India, Pares Back Environmental Rules
2014-12-05.abstract: The new government is moving with remarkable speed to clear away regulatory burdens for industry, the armed forces, mining and power projects.

2014-12-05.title: Liberia Bans Election Rallies to Fight Ebola
2014-12-05.abstract: In issuing the crowd-control order, President Ellen Johnson Sirleaf argued that large concentrations of people at election rallies were precisely the situations that could spawn new infections.

2014-12-05.title: Meeting Afghan Leaders, Donors Pledge Support
2014-12-05.abstract: The promises came during an international conference at which American and British leaders praised Afghan President Ashraf Ghani’s unity government.

2014-12-06.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry warned Senator Dianne Feinstein that the release of the long-delayed review could ignite unrest in the Middle East and endanger hostages.

2014-12-06.abstract: Jurf al-Sakhar was recaptured from the Islamic State, but now Shiite-dominated forces say Sunnis cannot return to their homes for at least eight months.

2014-12-06.title: Mexico: Police Chief Says He Is Stepping Down
2014-12-06.abstract: Under fire over accusations of police brutality, Mexico City’s police chief, Jesús Rodríguez Almeida, resigned on Friday.

2014-12-06.abstract: Local militants have claimed responsibility for an attack that killed 14 police officers and at least one civilian, easing concerns that the Islamic State had been involved.

2014-12-06.abstract: Matthew and Grace Huang of Los Angeles faced a two-year legal struggle after the death of their adopted daughter.

2014-12-07.title: As Ebola Rages, Poor Planning Thwarts Efforts
2014-12-07.abstract: Aid officials in Sierra Leone say weak coordination among aid groups, government mismanagement and glaring inefficiencies are costing countless lives.

2014-12-07.title: Back to a Burmese Prison by Choice
2014-12-07.abstract: U Htein Lin was detained for more than six years in Myanmar as a political prisoner, but has recently returned to teach meditation to the prisoners and the guards.

2014-12-07.title: Facing Rights Accusations, Israel Opens Gaza Inquiries
2014-12-07.abstract: The Israeli military ordered eight additional criminal investigations into the conduct of its forces during the war this summer in the Gaza Strip.

2014-12-07.title: Eyeing Return, Ex-President in France Has Hurdles
2014-12-07.abstract: Nicolas Sarkozy’s first week as leader of the Union for a Popular Movement Party seemed to prove only that he had determined rivals within the party and was not assured of becoming its presidential candidate.

2014-12-07.title: Remains of Student in Mexico Identified
2014-12-07.abstract: A lab’s finding lends credence to a theory that the police abducted 43 students and turned them over to a drug gang, which killed them.

2014-12-08.abstract: The reason for the government’s sudden shift, analysts say, was that Zaki Bani Rushaid crossed a political line by lashing out at the United Arab Emirates, an ally.

2014-12-08.title: A Sikh Principal, Too English for a Largely Muslim School
2014-12-08.abstract: Balwant Bains was at the center of the issues facing multicultural Britain, including the perennial question of balancing religious precepts and cultural identity against assimilation.

2014-12-08.abstract: Despite its rich culture, its memory of war and its present-day prosperity, Dresden is no stranger to right-wingers or hatred of foreigners.

2014-12-08.title: Germany’s Refugee Crisis
2014-12-08.abstract: More refugees are seeking asylum in Germany than in any other country, straining Germans’ tolerance for foreigners and taxing the government’s ability to find housing for them.

2014-12-08.abstract: The project, called Initiative 20x20, intends to restore about 49 million acres of degraded land — an area larger than Uruguay — including about 21 million acres in Mexico.

2014-12-09.title: Allies to Bolster U.S. Troops Advising Iraqis
2014-12-09.abstract: The combined number of military advisers helping Iraq forces in their fight against the Islamic State would be 4,500.

2014-12-09.title: A ‘Black Widow’ Case Strikes a Nerve in Japan
2014-12-09.abstract: The sudden death of a newlywed 75-year-old man had led to suspicions that his wife, Chisako Kakehi, may have killed as many as seven men.

2014-12-09.title: Palestinians Become Observers at Meeting on International Criminal Court
2014-12-09.abstract: Their attendance at the Assembly of States Parties at the United Nations is seen as symbolic but significant in light of the Palestinians’ hopes for statehood.

2014-12-09.abstract: The prime minister of India has a complicated past with Muslims, who make up a majority of the troubled region. So an uneventful campaign stop came as a surprise.

2014-12-09.abstract: The World Health Organization reported steep declines in malaria cases and deaths compared with 2000 in a report released early Tuesday, saying the progress was particularly notable in Africa, where the disease is most prevalent.

2014-12-10.abstract: Jihadist web postings called for retaliation, and the United States warned citizens in Thailand and Afghanistan about hostile reactions.

2014-12-10.abstract: Secretary of State John Kerry on Tuesday told Congress that an authorization of use of military force (A.U.M.F.) against the Islamic State should not restrict military operations to Syria and Iraq.

2014-12-10.title: Abu Dhabi Investors Buy Scotland Yard
2014-12-10.abstract: The new owners are expected to transform the property into apartments, office space and a hotel by 2017. The police force will move to Victoria Embankment.

2014-12-10.title: With Compromises, a Global Accord to Fight Climate Change Is in Sight
2014-12-10.abstract: A draft climate accord being circulating among negotiators would have every country pledge to curb fossil fuels, but each would set its own goals.

2014-12-10.title: Evidence of C.I.A. Abuses Prompts Global #TortureReport Discussion
2014-12-10.abstract: Journalists and bloggers around the world parsed the Senate torture report on Twitter, highlighting details and sharing reactions.

2014-12-11.title: Alliance Adds Twist to Israeli Elections
2014-12-11.abstract: Isaac Herzog, the leader of Israel’s center-left Labor Party, and Tzipi Livni, the recently dismissed justice minister, announced on Wednesday that they would form an alliance to challenge Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in elections next March.

2014-12-11.abstract: On Thursday, the Hong Kong police moved in to clear away the encampment of pro-democracy protesters who had occupied streets near government offices for 10 weeks.

2014-12-11.abstract: The two nations were bound throughout the Cold War by a mutual mistrust of the United States, though there is now some uncertainty in the old relationship.

2014-12-11.abstract: The Amazon has long been a conduit for cocaine trafficked from the Andes to the Atlantic. But increasingly, drugs have been diverted to Manaus leading to a surge in crime and violence.

2014-12-11.abstract: The New York Times has filed a request that the Justice Department disclose documents from an investigation into C.I.A. torture of detainees, including summaries of interviews with witnesses and documents explaining why no charges were filed.

2014-12-12.abstract: At an ashram in Punjab, followers of Ashutosh Maharaj insist that he is in a state of higher consciousness. A court disagrees, and has ordered his body cremated.

2014-12-12.title: Ivory Coast: International Court Wants to Try Ex-President’s Wife
2014-12-12.abstract: The International Criminal Court on Thursday ordered Ivory Coast to hand over the country’s former first lady, Simone Gbagbo, to face charges of crimes against humanity.

2014-12-12.title: Obama Favors Sanctions for Abuse of Venezuela Protesters
2014-12-12.abstract: The president plans to approve penalties against officials in Venezuela who violated the rights of antigovernment demonstrators in that country, a spokesman said.

2014-12-12.title: Dogs in Heaven? Pope Francis Leaves Pearly Gates Open
2014-12-12.abstract: Pope Francis’ remarks to a distraught little boy whose dog had died have caused a new debate on whether animals have souls, suffer and go to heaven.

2014-12-12.abstract: In founding The Phnom Penh Post in 1992, Ms. O’Keefe introduced objective journalism in a still-tumultuous Cambodia.

2014-12-13.abstract: The prosecutor of the International Criminal Court said Friday that she was suspending investigations in Darfur because no arrests had been made in a decade and the United Nations Security Council had been unwilling to use its powers to help.

2014-12-13.abstract: A special prosecutor to investigate corruption is expected to be named soon, but falling confidence in the president and the political process itself is imperiling the mission before it begins.

2014-12-13.abstract: Of 119 prisoners in C.I.A custody, the Senate report found that 26 were either described in the agency’s own documents as mistakenly detained, or released and given money.

2014-12-13.abstract: The counteroffensive, which includes a new website, began even before the Senate committee issued its report on the agency’s harsh treatment of detainees.

2014-12-14.abstract: Mr. Oakley was best known for negotiating the release of an American pilot held by Somali militiamen in 1993 in what was known as the “Black Hawk Down” crisis.

2014-12-14.abstract: Liberia’s Supreme Court on Saturday said it would not halt Senate elections scheduled for Tuesday, but the ultimate day of the vote remains unclear.

2014-12-14.title: How to Help Sweetie Sweetie
2014-12-14.abstract: The home where Sweetie Sweetie is staying is sponsored by ChildFund International, a private aid agency.

2014-12-14.abstract: After her mother died of Ebola, a young girl in Sierra Leone was left an orphan when nobody in her village, even relatives, wanted to take her, though she showed no symptoms of the virus.

2014-12-15.abstract: Police officers surrounded the cafe in the central business district where hostages held a flag with writing that appeared to be the shahada, the Muslim declaration of faith.

2014-12-15.abstract: Sony’s executives now say they knew that basing a film on the assassination of Kim Jong-un of North Korea had inherent risks.

2014-12-16.abstract: The mayor of Paris, Anne Hidalgo, has proposed banning high-emission vehicles, particularly those that run on diesel, in the city’s center by 2020.

2014-12-16.abstract: At least 43 deaths were reported in Monday’s assaults, which were led by the Nusra Front, and at least 15 Syrian soldiers were said to have been captured.

2014-12-17.abstract: The Pakistani Taliban’s attack on a school in Peshawar in December signaled a significant change of tactics.

2014-12-17.abstract: The trip, which is to begin later this week, seems designed to send a message of solidarity with the three affected countries: Guinea, Liberia and Sierra Leone.

2014-12-18.abstract: By Wednesday evening, amid indications of a government intervention in the currency markets, the ruble had recovered more than 11 percent of its previous day’s losses.

2014-12-18.abstract: The border police, whose green berets are a nod to the Green Line that demarcated Israeli territory from its hostile neighbors in 1949 armistice agreements, serve in the most sensitive situations.

2014-12-18.abstract: The release of Mr. Gross, who was detained on Dec. 3, 2009, and labeled a spy, was part of a historic thaw in Cuban-American relations announced by President Obama.

2014-12-18.abstract: With flags at half-staff and businesses shuttered, Pakistanis seemed to be trapped between grief, anger and frustration, for once overcoming their divisions to unite in pain.

2014-12-19.abstract: Tunisian fighters with the Islamic State group have claimed responsibility in a video for the assassinations of two left-wing politicians.

2014-12-19.abstract: Seeking to justify his foreign policy as essentially defensive, Russia’s president cited apocryphal remarks wrongly attributed to Madeleine Albright.

2014-12-19.abstract: Boko Haram, the extremist group that operates widely in the volatile northeastern part of the country, is suspected of being responsible.

2014-12-19.abstract: Mr. Rubio, Republican of Florida and a likely presidential candidate, will find out on just which side of the polls — and history — he stands.

2014-12-20.title: Colombia: A Clash Before a Truce
2014-12-20.abstract: Colombian guerrillas killed five government soldiers in a clash on Friday, the Defense Ministry said.

2014-12-20.title: Turkey Issues Arrest Warrant for a Rival of Its President
2014-12-20.abstract: The cleric facing arrest, Fethullah Gulen, lives in Pennsylvania but still has a broad following in Turkey that has reached into elite law enforcement, judiciary and business circles.

2014-12-20.abstract: President Obama signed an executive order barring new investment and trade with Crimea and freezing the property of people or businesses operating in the region or helping its leadership.

2014-12-20.abstract: The collapse of the Russian ruble — down more than 45 percent this year — has jangled nerves in Russia, but the reverberations are being felt in tourist destinations across Europe.

2014-12-20.title: Crucible of Cuban Zeal Redefines Revolutionary
2014-12-20.abstract: Cárdenas, Cuba, which is home to Elián González, is a growing city of contrasts and contradictions.

2014-12-21.abstract: In a plot that seems lifted from an international thriller, a former Army sniper who became entangled with an arms dealer now stands accused of taking a job as a hired killer.

2014-12-21.abstract: The lack of unity is raising questions about the ability of the gulf states to muster a coherent response to a storm of crises.

2014-12-21.abstract: A retired general’s scathing account of North Korea as a recalcitrant ally headed for collapse and unworthy of China’s support revealed how far relations between the two countries have sunk.

2014-12-21.abstract: Tokyo Electric Power Company passed one milestone, but faces the far more challenging task of removing the ruined fuel cores from the three reactors that melted down.

2014-12-21.title: 1914: France Silences Enemy Guns Near Arras
2014-12-21.abstract: From the International Herald Tribune archives: After intense German shelling near Arras in 1914, the French fight back.

2014-12-22.abstract: A centuries-old Christmas tradition called the Krampus Run returns to Bavaria where monsters roam the streets in search of bad children.

2014-12-22.title: Missed Signals Before the Mumbai Attacks
2014-12-22.abstract: Intelligence agencies in three countries tracked conspirators and deployed high-tech surveillance tools in advance of the Mumbai attacks, but they did not put the pieces together.

2014-12-22.abstract: An investigation reveals how American, British and Indian agencies failed to pull together intelligence streams, including a plotter’s online activity, before attacks that Indians regard as their 9/11.

2014-12-22.abstract: In Germany, misbehaving children had better watch out. The Krampus, a devilish goblin who tears through the streets as St. Nick’s evil counterpart, is making a comeback.

2014-12-23.title: Taliban Push Into Afghan Districts That U.S. Had Secured
2014-12-23.abstract: Just four years after the American troop surge, a sustained fighting effort by the Taliban has put parts of southern Afghanistan back under direct threat.

2014-12-23.abstract: The City Council in Chivilcoy, Argentina, has banned beauty pageants after it decided that the nation’s unwavering expectations for female beauty were out of control.

2014-12-23.abstract: The former soccer star George Weah appeared to have won a landslide victory in a Senate race that pitted him against the president’s son, Robert Sirleaf.

2014-12-23.abstract: The shutdown of the channel, an Egyptian affiliate of the Qatari-owned Al Jazeera network, is also a blow to what remains of the Muslim Brotherhood.

2014-12-23.title: A Window Onto Evolving Traditions in Paris
2014-12-23.abstract: The Christmas window displays at Paris’s main department stores have bent obligingly with the years, reflecting a creeping — sometimes jarring — fusion of popular and consumer culture.

2014-12-24.abstract: Amir Hekmati, of Flint, Mich., has been incarcerated for more than three years and is the longest-serving among the three Americans of Iranian descent known to be jailed by Iran.

2014-12-24.title: Opting to Avoid a Detour, Passengers Brave a War Zone’s Skies
2014-12-24.abstract: The chaos on the ground in Syria didn’t appear to faze the passengers aboard a flight from Beirut to Amman.

2014-12-24.title: Hong Kong: 2 Sentenced in Graft Case
2014-12-24.abstract: The former No. 2 official in the Hong Kong government was sentenced to seven and a half years in prison and a property tycoon to five years on Tuesday.

2014-12-24.title: After Long Fight, Groups Pry Memo on Migrant Killings From Mexican Government
2014-12-24.abstract: In a small victory for transparency advocates, Mexico released a document from the attorney general’s office that describes police complicity in the killings of migrants by drug gangs.

2014-12-24.title: Cuba’s Economic Fortunes May be Slow to Turn
2014-12-24.abstract: President Obama’s plan to allow more interaction may not be the lifeline Cuba is hoping for — unless Cuba overcomes its resistance to change as well.

2014-12-25.title: Germans Balk at Plan for Wind Power Lines
2014-12-25.abstract: Germans have learned to love all things green, but a plan to build hundreds of miles of high-voltage direct current lines is too much for some.

2014-12-25.title: Afghan Leader Tells U.N. Agency to Relinquish Control of Funds, Officials Say
2014-12-25.abstract: Officials said President Ashraf Ghani is frustrated with what he sees as mismanagement of the United Nations Development Program, which bankrolls the salaries of Afghanistan’s police officers.

2014-12-25.title: China: Monk Burns Himself to Death in a Political Protest
2014-12-25.abstract: A Tibetan monk died Tuesday after setting himself on fire near his monastery in Sichuan Province, Tibetan exile and advocacy groups said Wednesday.

2014-12-25.title: Israel: Politicians Are Detained in a Broad Corruption Inquiry
2014-12-25.abstract: More than 30 Israeli politicians and public figures have been detained for questioning in connection with a corruption probe, the police said Wednesday.

2014-12-25.title: Oil’s Swift Fall Raises Fortunes of U.S. Abroad
2014-12-25.abstract: A plunge in oil prices has sent tremors through the global political and economic order, helping the United States but pushing several big oil-exporting nations to the brink of financial crisis.

2014-12-26.title: For Sri Lankan President, Renounced by Aides, Confidence of Re-election Dims
2014-12-26.abstract: Defectors from the cabinet of President Mahinda Rajapaksa have bragged about plotting their desertion under his nose.

2014-12-26.title: Decline of a Political Family Opens the Way for a Shift in Brazil
2014-12-26.abstract: As José Sarney leaves Brazil’s Senate, his family dynasty in Maranhão State is showing signs of strain.

2014-12-26.title: U.N. Set to Cut Force in Darfur as Fighting Rises
2014-12-26.abstract: United Nations officials say their forces are routinely attacked by Sudanese forces and their proxies and that it is virtually impossible for their peacekeepers to remain without Sudan’s blessing.

2014-12-26.title: Chinese Viewers Mostly Give Thumbs Up for ‘The Interview’
2014-12-26.abstract: By midday on Friday, more than 300,000 people in China had seen the film about a plot to assassinate Kim Jong-un of North Korea and the reviews, by and large, were favorable.

2014-12-26.title: Loss by Taiwan’s Governing Party Raises Fears That Ties With China Will Sour
2014-12-26.abstract: When the opposition Democratic Progressive Party last held power, it presided over a period of increased tensions with the mainland.

2014-12-27.title: After Scrutiny, C.I.A. Mandate Is Untouched
2014-12-27.abstract: Four decades ago, a Senate committee’s revelations about abuses committed by the intelligence community led to major alterations at the C.I.A. But times have changed.

2014-12-27.title: Human Rights Groups in Egypt Brace for Crackdown Under New Law
2014-12-27.abstract: Advocates worry about how prosecutors will apply a sweeping law decreed by the new military-backed president as a tool to fight terrorism.

2014-12-27.title: Sudden U.S. Thaw Worries Cuban Dissidents
2014-12-27.abstract: As the United States and Cuba enter a period of unprecedented dialogue, many dissidents who stood with American officials in condemning the Castros contend that President Obama gave away too much.

2014-12-27.title: Even Loyalty No Guarantee Against Putin
2014-12-27.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia, who wields virtually unchallenged power to reward a new class of oligarchs, also uses the same instrument to punish those who oppose him.

2014-12-27.title: Mexico: Body of Kidnapped Priest Is Found
2014-12-27.abstract: State prosecutors in Guerrero opened an investigation Friday into the slaying of the Rev. Gregorio López Gorostieta, whose body was found on Christmas Day.

2014-12-28.title: As Crisis Saps Economy, Belarus Replaces Premier
2014-12-28.abstract: President Aleksandr G. Lukashenko of Belarus dismissed his prime minister and the head of the country’s central bank on Saturday as part of a sweeping and unexpected government reorganization.

2014-12-28.title: The Cost of the U.S. Ban on Paying for Hostages
2014-12-28.abstract: Former hostages and Syrian rebel commanders say that they offered information they believed could help free American captives, but that officials seemed uninterested.

2014-12-28.title: AirAsia Flight Said to Be Lost in Indonesia
2014-12-28.abstract: Flight QZ8501 was en route from the Indonesian city of Surabaya to Singapore when it requested a “deviation” from its usual flight path because of weather, the airline said. The plane was carrying 155 passengers and seven crew members.

2014-12-28.title: Indonesian Agency Says Missing AirAsia Jet Probably Sank
2014-12-28.abstract: The head of the agency searching for Flight 8501 said that he believed the aircraft was “at the bottom of the sea” and warned that the country lacked adequate equipment for an underwater search.

2014-12-28.title: Where AirAsia Flight 8501 Was Lost and Debris Found
2014-12-28.abstract: Maps of the flight path, air traffic near the plane when it disappeared and where debris was found.

2014-12-29.title: Where Afghan War Was Transferred Long Ago
2014-12-29.abstract: The official conclusion of the international combat mission in Afghanistan arrived Sunday, but for the Afghans fighting in this remote border district, the symbolic end to America’s 13-year war brought little change.

2014-12-29.title: Around an Invisible Leader, Taliban Power Shifts
2014-12-29.abstract: The reclusiveness of Mullah Muhammad Omar raises questions about the group’s leadership and its influence in the jihadist world next to the Islamic State.

2014-12-29.title: Radio Station Backed by U.S. Is Raided in Azerbaijan
2014-12-29.abstract: A dozen employees of Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty, including an anchor and the cleaning woman, were arrested and detained for up to 12 hours over the weekend, as prosecutors intensified a crackdown on journalists.

2014-12-29.title: Hamas Turns Back 37 Gaza War Orphans From a Bridge-Building Trip to Israel
2014-12-29.abstract: Israeli and Palestinian officials had organized the visit for 37 children from Gaza as a way to promote peace and reconciliation, but the Hamas authorities turned them back at a border crossing.

2014-12-29.title: In Battle to Defang ISIS, U.S. Targets Its Psychology
2014-12-29.abstract: Officials acknowledge they have barely made a dent in the larger, longer-term campaign to kill the ideology that animates the terrorist movement.

2014-12-30.title: An Economic Boom Recedes, but South America Might Avert the Bust
2014-12-30.abstract: Governments of Brazil, Peru and Venezuela are seen as less likely than before to load up on debt and ignore danger signs.

2014-12-30.title: Britain: Ebola Case Reported in Glasgow
2014-12-30.abstract: A woman who had been working in Sierra Leone was being treated Monday for Ebola in a Glasgow hospital.

2014-12-30.title: Guards Retreat in London, but Just a Bit
2014-12-30.abstract: At some residences for the queen’s family, the guards have moved behind gates, and farther from tourists, because of concerns about terrorism.

2014-12-30.title: Austerity, From Street Level in Greece
2014-12-30.abstract: The vast majority of Greeks are still experiencing the hardships of reform, with little evidence that the measures are working.

2014-12-30.title: Greek Patience With Austerity Nears Its Limit
2014-12-30.abstract: The vast majority of Greeks are still experiencing the hardships of reform, with little evidence that the measures are working.

2014-12-31.title: Source of Ebola Outbreak in West Africa Might Be Bats, Study Says
2014-12-31.abstract: Scientists have traced the origins of the current epidemic to a toddler in Guinea who may have caught the virus from bats in a tree near his village.

2014-12-31.title: Text of Draft Palestinian Resolution
2014-12-31.abstract: The draft, edited by diplomats from Arab countries and Palestinian diplomats, sets a one-year deadline for negotiations with Israel and sets down targets for Palestinian sovereignty, including “full and phased withdrawal of Israeli forces” from the West Bank by the end of 2017.

2014-12-31.title: Ebola Ravages Economies in West Africa
2014-12-31.abstract: In Sierra Leone, Guinea and Liberia, Ebola is not just affecting immune systems but also the bottom lines of businesses and governments.

2014-12-31.title: Canada: Man Kills Eight in Alberta and Then Commits Suicide, Police Say
2014-12-31.abstract: A man killed eight people, including two young children, in a “senseless mass murder” in Alberta before committing suicide, the Edmonton police chief, Rod Knecht, said Tuesday.

2014-12-31.title: Drone Strike in Somalia Is Said to Kill Shabab Leader
2014-12-31.abstract: The American strike killed the chief of intelligence for the Shabab, the Qaeda affiliate, Somali officials said. American officials have not identified the target.
